Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal function. Urine is cloudy, brown, with ammoniacal odor, and contains ketones and bilirubin, but no leukocytes or nitrites, making infection less likely. Blood glucose and HbA1c are not elevated, so diabetes is unlikely. Elevated chloride may indicate mild metabolic disturbance. Recommend nephrology evaluation for possible chronic kidney disease and further assessment of urinary abnormalities.
